channel.title: Comments for page "Do They Know It's Christmas Time At All?"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Do They Know It's Christmas Time At All?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finished reading just in time for Christmas! :D

You do good work, Fishtruck. The pacing felt a little fast in places, but the story overall is not only a perfect fit for Wonderful World, but damn good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this in the forums! Good to see it on the mainsite. Lots of lovely worldbuilding and character exploration here.

+1 and a happy Christmas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might edit this post later to fill you with even more praise, but damn did I love this. Isabel made me cackle and I hope she appears again.

Also, this is the perfect way to keep the site’s most popular Wondertainment interpretation (Isabel Parvati) while not breaking the lore of What a Wonderful World. It’s jolly perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a heartwarming as heck holiday story, plus a fun look inside the some of the ways OW interacts with outsiders. One of the takeaways for me was, this is an example of how the citizens of Wonder World are dealing with their grief following the Brainy Brian fiasco: thinking a little bit harder about all that's bad in the world and reaffirming their commitment to make it better if they can. Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've said this before and I will gladly say it again: you have NAILED the Wonder World!™ aesthetic.

item.title: I Like Those J-I-N-G-L-E Bells, woah-oh
item.content:encoded: Here's a Christmasy tale for a Christmany month, featuring Wonder World!™, the Factory, and Oneiroi. I'd like to thank Uncle Nicolini, Zyn, some fellows on the SCPD Discord, some fellows on the IRC chat, and of course DarkStuff for creating the Wonderful World Canon. I hope you enjoyed it!
